description: End-to-end streaming works without buffering across full chain
steps: Enable provider mode in chatbot settings
Send a chat message from frontend widget
Verify tokens appear incrementally in the UI (not all at once)
Verify no perceptible delay between provider receiving OpenAI tokens and forwarding them
Verify long responses stream smoothly without stalling
Verify streaming works identically to direct OpenAI mode from user perspective

description: Admin toggle switches between provider mode and direct OpenAI mode
steps: Open chatbot admin settings
Verify toggle exists for provider/direct mode
Set to direct mode and verify chatbot connects to OpenAI directly (requires API key)
Set to provider mode and verify chatbot routes through provider
Verify direct mode still works as before (backwards compatible)
Verify switching modes does not lose other settings
Verify provider mode does not require user to enter their own OpenAI key

description: Provider completion loop in chatbot has a max iteration limit to prevent infinite recursion when AI keeps returning tool_calls without a final text response.
steps: Trigger provider mode with a request that causes tool calls
Verify normal tool-call loops (1-3 iterations) complete successfully
Simulate a scenario where every provider response contains tool_calls (never text)
Verify the loop stops after a reasonable max (e.g. 10 iterations)
Verify an error message is sent to the frontend when max iterations exceeded
Verify no PHP fatal errors or memory exhaustion occur

description: Chatbot checks HTTP status code after fopen() to the provider, so auth failures (403), server errors (500), etc. produce meaningful error messages instead of a generic 'Failed to connect'.
steps: Send a request with an invalid provider_site_key
Verify the error message distinguishes auth failure from connection failure
Simulate a provider returning 500
Verify the error message indicates a server error
Verify $http_response_header is inspected after fopen()
Verify the specific HTTP status code is included or mapped to a human-readable message

description: Stream read failures in chatbot's stream_from_provider() are handled gracefully — fread() returning false closes the stream and sends an error to the frontend instead of silently breaking.
steps: Simulate fread() returning false mid-stream
Verify the stream is closed (fclose called)
Verify an error is returned to the caller (not silent break)
Verify the frontend receives an error event, not a truncated response

description: Provider clears all output buffer levels before streaming SSE, not just the top-level buffer. Other plugins/themes may add nested buffers that corrupt the SSE stream.
steps: Simulate multiple nested output buffers (ob_start called 3+ times)
Trigger the provider SSE endpoint
Verify all buffer levels are cleared (while loop, not single if)
Verify SSE output is clean with no HTML/whitespace prepended

description: Provider validates request size limits — max number of messages, max content length per message, and max number of tools — to prevent resource exhaustion and cost explosion.
steps: Send a request with more than 200 messages
Verify it is rejected with a clear error
Send a request with a single message over 100KB content
Verify it is rejected with a clear error
Send a request with more than 50 tools
Verify it is rejected with a clear error
Verify normal-sized requests still pass validation

description: Provider restricts which OpenAI models clients can request. Unrecognized or expensive models are rejected to prevent cost escalation.
steps: Send a request with a whitelisted model (e.g. gpt-4o-mini)
Verify it succeeds
Send a request with an unknown model string
Verify it is rejected with an error
Send a request with no model specified
Verify it falls back to the default model (not rejected)
Verify the allowed models list is configurable via settings or a constant

description: Provider sanitizes error messages before streaming to clients. Internal details (file paths, API key fragments, stack traces) must not leak through SSE error events.
steps: Trigger an OpenAI SDK exception (e.g. invalid API key)
Verify the SSE error event does not contain file paths or stack traces
Trigger a network timeout exception
Verify the error message is generic (e.g. 'OpenAI request failed') not the raw cURL error
Verify known OpenAI error types (rate limit, auth) map to safe messages
